headline: $EQT bullish — largest US natgas producer lagging peers despite cold weather surge
report:
Catalyst:
Natural gas prices are surging on extended cold weather forecasts, with Henry Hub pushing above $3.50. The forecast shows sustained below-normal temperatures across key demand regions through mid-January, driving heating demand and storage draws. This matters for EQT because they are the largest US natural gas producer with 95% revenue exposure to natgas pricing.

Why this name:
EQT offers the cleanest pure-play natural gas exposure among liquid large-cap E&Ps. While peers like Antero Resources and Range Resources have meaningful NGL or oil revenue, EQT's business is almost entirely tied to dry gas production and Henry Hub pricing.

What already moved vs what has not:
Antero Resources is up 8% and Range Resources up 5% on the same natural gas catalyst. EQT has only moved 1.5%, with the market apparently treating it like a diversified E&P when it has the highest natgas revenue concentration. This 3-4% lag creates the setup.

Tape:
Fresh call buying is evident at $62.50-$65.00 strikes with 2-3x normal volume, and open interest is building on the weekly cycle. Dark-pool activity shows moderate block buying with two $2M+ prints on the bid side. Dealers are short gamma above $62, so a move through $63 could accelerate. The tape is supportive rather than euphoric.

Catalyst health:
The cold weather catalyst is strengthening. Extended forecasts continue showing below-normal temperatures, and storage data is confirming accelerated draws. The setup remains intact.

Timing:
Window is 1-2 weeks as weather patterns typically resolve within 10-14 days and natgas prices respond quickly to forecast revisions. Storage reports and daily weather updates provide regular catalyst refresh.

Risk:
Thesis breaks if Henry Hub drops below $3.00 on warm weather forecast revisions, which would eliminate the heating demand premium driving current pricing.

Why ORCA likes it:
This setup combines a visible weather-driven catalyst with a clean single-name expression and measurable underperformance versus the underlying driver. The tape confirms fresh interest rather than momentum chasing, and EQT's pure-play exposure offers direct participation in any natgas repricing without sector rotation confusion.
